center.name: 臺拉維夫大學
center.summary: 臺拉維夫大學是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Some populations adhere to the eastern migration route, which passes across Israel into eastern and central Africa. In Africa the white stork may spend the winter in Tunisia, Morocco, Uganda, Angola, Zimbabwe, Djibouti, Botswana, Mozambique, Zambia, Eswatini, Gambia, Guinea, Algeria, and Ghana. A few records of breeding from South Africa have been known since 1933 at Calitzdorp, and about 10 birds have been known to breed since the 1990s around Bredasdorp.{{cite book |pages= 82–3 |title= The Atlas of Southern African Birds |volume=1: Non-passerines |last= Allan |first= David G
